summary: This brief post announces O'Neil's Bloomberg View column arguing against using Value-Added Models (VAM) to evaluate teachers. She signals this is among her most important work on the topic, suggesting the algorithmic approach to teacher grading may finally be losing credibility. The post serves primarily as a pointer to her longer argument about why these statistical models are fundamentally flawed for high-stakes personnel decisions.
key_points: Value-Added Models for teacher evaluation are deeply problematic algorithms
O'Neil considers this critique among her most important Bloomberg columns
The title suggests optimism that VAM may be losing institutional support
money_quotes: The VAM Might Finally be Dead
Don't Grade Teachers With a Bad Algorithm
key_insight: Algorithmic teacher evaluation models are bad algorithms that shouldn't be used for high-stakes grading decisions.
